observed_failure.surface: SkillGuard v0.3.2 candidate transactional synchronization to the active Codex installation
observed_failure.prior_local_claim: a validated replacement stage can recover an old committed installation and activate transactionally
observed_failure.observed_result: the active v0.3.1-era tree carried a manifest compiled under an older template-hash projection; current projection replay raised installation_projection_component_hash_mismatch and crashed recovery before replacement activation

classification.error_signature: installation_projection_identity raises while recovery evaluates the committed HEAD

root_cause_backpropagation.supported_cause: the recovery model distinguished active installed identity from evolving canonical source, but the implementation assumed active projection replay always returned a value and did not model a fail-closed projection exception
root_cause_backpropagation.would_have_failed_if: the model had included a committed active tree whose stored bytes remain available but whose embedded component hashes are not recomputable under the current projection policy
root_cause_backpropagation.new_model_item: an active projection replay error makes the old committed head non-current for publication, but a separately exact stored byte identity keeps it recoverably sound for direct validated replacement; byte drift still enters rollback
root_cause_backpropagation.owner_code_contract: installation._activation_receipt_active_current owns semantic currentness; installation._activation_receipt_active_recoverable owns stored-byte rollback integrity

generalized_repair.rule: Separate semantic currentness from stored-byte recovery integrity: policy-stale projection errors cannot satisfy currentness, but do not roll back an otherwise exact committed HEAD before a verified replacement; actual byte drift still follows rollback.
generalized_repair.observed_regression: a mocked current-policy projection error leaves the exact committed HEAD in place, while a separate active-byte drift case returns non-current and is recovered before a fresh replacement activates
generalized_repair.same_class_regression: policy-stale and byte-drift projections are both non-current but have distinct recovery dispositions based on exact stored identity
generalized_repair.negative_boundary: the old active tree does not become publication-current, no compatibility reader is added, no manifest is rewritten in place, byte drift does not pass, and the incoming stage must still pass exact parity and smoke checks

required_revalidation: current contract compilation and FlowGuard validation composition
real transactional reinstall and installed receipt parity
frozen-commit Windows and Ubuntu GitHub Actions

revalidation_evidence.focused_recovery: the policy-stale projection replacement regression and active-byte-drift replacement regression passed
revalidation_evidence.installation_module: 19 installation tests and 6 subtests passed before the final status-normalization adjustment; the exact added status-normalization regression passed afterward
revalidation_evidence.affected_release_shard: 60 passed, 1 capability-dependent case skipped, and 30 subtests passed across all affected release modules after the final repair
revalidation_evidence.model_and_contract: validation composition passed 19/19 positive and 7/7 known-bad gates; contract 325507B1F9D1D26EFB484AB950879EA828F1C239BABEB9D685E8470E54E990CC and manifest 0F140F0571CA948B3137CED347D174730E59A126FC1A43354464D7D5BD20D1FC compiled current
revalidation_evidence.real_installation: transaction install-e20fbe62e4994e42a2c081a22e33f154 replaced the policy-stale generation and committed as generation 36 with all installed smoke checks passed
revalidation_evidence.installed_receipt: installation-05f5d47eb06c449e10782f97 replayed current canonical-to-active parity with no blockers

claim_boundary: This record proves the named local recovery repair, affected regressions, current model/contract compilation, real transactional replacement, and installed parity receipt. Remote Windows/Ubuntu CI, the corrective tag, and GitHub Release remain pending claims.
